user.title: Particle Man
user.description: Particle Man uses Google's air quality, pollen, and weather data. You'll need a Google Maps Platform API key with the Air Quality, Pollen, and Weather APIs enabled. [Get an API key →]({docs_url})

data_description.api_key: Paste your Google Maps Platform key here — it won't be visible after saving. [How to get a key]({docs_url})
data_description.name: A name for this location (e.g. Home, Work). You can add more locations after setup.
data_description.latitude: Decimal latitude of the location to monitor. Pre-filled from Home Assistant.
data_description.longitude: Decimal longitude of the location to monitor. Pre-filled from Home Assistant.

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate Particle Man
reauth_confirm.description: Your API key is no longer valid. Enter a new Google Maps Platform API key to restore access.

data_description.api_key: Paste your new Google Maps Platform key here.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Particle Man
reconfigure.description: Update the Google Maps Platform API key for this integration.

data_description.api_key: Paste your updated Google Maps Platform key here.

error.invalid_auth: API key not recognized — make sure the Air Quality, Pollen, and Weather APIs are enabled in your Google Cloud project.
error.cannot_connect: Couldn't reach Google. Check your internet connection and try again.
error.unknown: Something went wrong. Check the Home Assistant logs for details.
error.aq_not_enabled: The Air Quality API is not enabled for this API key. Enable it in the Google Cloud Console.
error.pollen_not_enabled: The Pollen API is not enabled for this API key. Enable it in the Google Cloud Console.
error.weather_not_enabled: The Weather API is not enabled for this API key. Enable it in the Google Cloud Console.

abort.already_configured: An entry for this API key is already configured.

error.aq_not_enabled: The Air Quality API is not enabled for this API key. Enable it in the Google Cloud Console.
error.pollen_not_enabled: The Pollen API is not enabled for this API key. Enable it in the Google Cloud Console.
error.weather_not_enabled: The Weather API is not enabled for this API key. Enable it in the Google Cloud Console.
error.local_aqi_unavailable: This regional AQI standard is not available at your location. See the Google documentation for available standards.
error.location_name_duplicate: A location with that name already exists. Use a unique name.

init.title: API Mode
init.description:
Choose how Particle Man manages API calls.

**Automagic Free API Mode** keeps all enabled APIs within Google's free monthly quota automatically. In this mode:
- **Air quality and pollen** check for new data every hour, matching Google's data refresh schedule.
- **Weather** is checked more frequently — the interval is calculated from your location count and the free-tier limit.

Turn off Automagic to set your own update interval and monthly limits. [Free tier details]({api_usage_url})

data_description.automagic_mode: When on, Particle Man stays within Google's free quota automatically. Turn off to configure your own limits.

locations.title: Manage Locations
locations.description:
Each location gets its own set of sensors. All locations share the same API key and global settings.

{location_list}

data.action: What would you like to do?

add_location.title: Add Location
add_location.description: Enter the name and coordinates for the new location.{coverage_notes}

data_description.name: A label for this location (e.g. Home, Work, Cabin). Must be unique.
data_description.latitude: Decimal latitude of the location to monitor.
data_description.longitude: Decimal longitude of the location to monitor.

remove_location.title: Remove Location
remove_location.description: Select a location to remove. All sensors for that location will be deleted.

quiet_hours.title: Quiet Hours
quiet_hours.description:
Quiet hours pause all API calls during a set overnight window — useful when fresh data isn't needed and you want to conserve your monthly quota.

You can also toggle quiet hours on the fly using the **Quiet Hours** switch entity without re-entering these settings.

data_description.quiet_hours_enabled: Skip data checks during the window below.
data_description.quiet_start: Stop checking at this time each day.
data_description.quiet_end: Resume checking at this time. If earlier than the start time, the pause spans midnight.

data_description.enable_air_quality: Current AQI, pollutant levels (PM2.5, ozone, NO₂, and more), and multi-day forecasts. Updated hourly (Google's data refresh rate).
data_description.enable_pollen: Pollen index by type (grass, tree, weed) with forecasts and optional per-plant-species detail. Updated hourly.
data_description.enable_weather: Current conditions, hourly and daily forecasts, and active weather alerts.
data_description.update_interval: How often to fetch new weather data. Air quality and pollen always update hourly regardless of this setting — a shorter interval won't give you fresher AQ or pollen data. [How call counts work]({api_usage_url})

air_quality.title: Air Quality Options
air_quality.description: Customize what the air quality sensors report.{local_aqi_detail}

data_description.forecast_days: How many days of forecast data to include on each sensor (1 = today only, 5 = maximum).
data_description.language_code: Language for pollutant names and health guidance — use a two-letter code like en, es, or fr.
data_description.enable_local_aqi: Adds a second AQI sensor using your region's health authority scale (e.g., US AQI) alongside the always-present Universal AQI (UAQI). [UAQI vs. regional standards →]({sensors_url})
data_description.local_aqi_code: The AQI standard used in your area. US users should typically choose US AQI. [Regional standards reference]({config_url})

weather.title: Weather Options
weather.description: Customize the weather entity for this location.

data_description.weather_units: Temperature and wind speed units for raw sensor values. Home Assistant can convert units for display independently.
data_description.enable_weather_alerts: Creates three sensors: Alert Count (number of active warnings), Alert Highest Severity (MINOR/MODERATE/SEVERE/EXTREME), and Alert Event Types (comma-separated list of active alert codes). All show empty/0 when no alerts are active.

api_limits.title: Custom Monthly Limits
api_limits.description: {usage_summary} Each API pauses for the rest of the month when its limit is reached. [How to view current usage]({api_usage_url})

data_description.aq_monthly_limit: Maximum Air Quality checks per month. Google's free tier is 10,000 — set higher if you're on a paid plan. [Pricing details]({api_usage_url})
data_description.pollen_monthly_limit: Maximum Pollen checks per month. Google's free tier is 5,000.
data_description.weather_monthly_limit: Maximum Weather checks per month. Google's free tier is 10,000.

no_data_available.message: No data available from any enabled API.

api_quota_exhausted.title: {api_name} API quota exhausted
api_quota_exhausted.description: The {api_name} API monthly call quota has been reached. Data updates are paused until the next billing cycle. To resume sooner, increase the monthly limit in the integration options or disable Automagic mode.
